building_name: 474 Spadina Ave / 277 College St (Rexall corner building)
facade_colour: light gray facade panels with bright teal illuminated Rexall sign boxes and clear storefront glazing

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: light gray facade panels with bright teal illuminated Rexall sign boxes and clear storefront glazing
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Contemporary corner drugstore appears well maintained and brightly lit, with clean cladding and active retail use at night.
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor is mostly transparent retail glazing; upper level is largely blank except for a few openings and a billboard above neighboring massing.
photo_observations.door_details: Main glazed retail entrance sits near the corner with a second side-facing entrance along the Spadina frontage.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple flat parapet line with no ornament.
photo_observations.porch_details: Retail facade meets the sidewalk directly.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Visual emphasis comes from illuminated signage and the simplified modern corner volume.
photo_observations.storefront_description: A brightly lit Rexall wraps the Spadina and College corner with full-height glazing, illuminated teal sign bands, and a highly visible interior organized around the corner entry and escalator.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Rexall Drug Store and OPEN LATE signage are prominent.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Modern reclad retail frontage and illuminated sign program define the current appearance.
photo_observations.notes: Best shown in IMG_20260321_013138642_HDR.jpg and IMG_20260321_012924795_HDR.jpg.

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 6.1 metres across 2 storeys beneath a flat roof, consistent with its commercial typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 6, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances.

facade_description: 474 Spadina Ave / 277 College St (Rexall corner building) presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 6.1 metres across 2 storeys beneath a flat roof, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by mixed in a light gray facade panels with bright teal illuminated rexall sign boxes and clear storefront glazing palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 6, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Key storefront and facade features include cornice.
